formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Chemistry - Redox Reactions in JSON format:

[
  {
    "name": "Oxidation Number (O.N.) of an element in a compound",
    "formula": "O.N. of element = Number of valence electrons of the element",
    "variables": "valence electrons",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Used to determine the oxidation state of an element in a compound"
  },
  {
    "name": "Oxidation Number (O.N.) of an element in a polyatomic ion",
    "formula": "O.N. of element = Charge on the polyatomic ion + Number of valence electrons of the element",
    "variables": "charge on the polyatomic ion, valence electrons",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Used to determine the oxidation state of an element in a polyatomic ion"
  },
  {
    "name": "Rules for assigning Oxidation Numbers",
    "formula": "O.N. of F, O, N always -ve; O.N. of H always +1; O.N. of Group 1 elements always +1; O.N. of Group 2 elements always +2",
    "variables": "F, O, N, H, Group 1 elements, Group 2 elements",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Used to assign oxidation numbers to elements in a compound or polyatomic ion"
  },
  {
    "name": "Oxidation Number (O.N.) of an element in a compound with polyatomic ions",
    "formula": "O.N. of element in compound = O.N. of element in polyatomic ion + Charge on polyatomic ion",
    "variables": "O.N. of element in polyatomic ion, charge on polyatomic ion",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Used to determine the oxidation state of an element in a compound with polyatomic ions"
  },
  {
    "name": "Redox Reaction (Balanced Equation)",
    "formula": "Oxidation: LHS → RHS; Reduction: LHS → RHS",
    "variables": "Oxidation: LHS = Left Hand Side; RHS = Right Hand Side; Reduction: LHS = Left Hand Side; RHS = Right Hand Side",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Used to identify and balance redox reactions"
  },
  {
    "name": "Half-Reaction Method for Balancing Redox Reactions",
    "formula": "Separate the reaction into oxidation and reduction half-reactions; Balance each half-reaction separately; Check if the number of electrons lost in oxidation equals the number of electrons gained in reduction",
    "variables": "oxidation half-reaction, reduction half-reaction",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Used to balance redox reactions using the half-reaction method"
  },
  {
    "name": "Ion-Electron Method for Balancing Redox Reactions",
    "formula": "Oxidation: LHS → RHS; Reduction: LHS → RHS; Balance the ion and electron half-reactions separately; Check if the number of electrons lost in oxidation equals the number of electrons gained in reduction",
    "variables": "ion half-reaction, electron half-reaction",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Used to balance redox reactions using the ion-electron method"
  }
]

Note: The provided JSON array contains key statements and laws related to redox reactions, including the rules for assigning oxidation numbers, and the methods for balancing redox reactions.
